topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box arrived with a soft thud on my doorstep. It was wrapped in plain, cream-colored paper, tied with a simple piece of twine. My fingers, usually steady, trembled slightly as I lifted the lid. Inside, nestled in crinkled tissue, were a dozen perfect, deep purple irises. There was no note. Just the blooms, vibrant and fragrant, sitting in a bed of moss. I stared at them, my breath catching in my throat. Who would do this? A sudden, hollow feeling settled in my chest, making my palms sweat. I picked one up, the cool petals a stark contrast to the warmth rising in my cheeks.

[story 2]

I found the delivery tucked beside my laptop. A bouquet of sunflowers, beaming with a cheerful, almost blinding yellow. The arrangement was stunning, overflowing with sunshine. I picked it up, the stems surprisingly heavy, and my grip tightened instinctively. My eyes scanned the empty space where a message should have been, searching for any clue, any explanation. Silence hung heavy in the air of my apartment. A strange tightness constricted my jaw, and I took a slow, shallow breath, trying to process the sheer unexpectedness of the gesture. It felt like a deliberate disruption to my ordinary day.

[story 3]

When I opened the package, the scent of lilies immediately enveloped me. They were magnificent, pure white, their velvety texture seeming almost too delicate for the simple wrapping. I sat down heavily on the floor, the flowers resting in my lap. My gaze darted around the room, searching for the source of this unsolicited beauty. A low, involuntary sound escaped my lips—a soft, disbelieving murmur. My hands instinctively went up to cover my mouth, a gesture of complete bewilderment. This was entirely out of context, and the sheer lack of explanation left a strange, unsettled flutter beneath my ribs.
